title: barbacenia aurea
intro: Barbacenia aurea, also known as the golden broom, is a succulent plant species native to Brazil, characterized by its yellow flowers and dense, branched stems. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Barbacenia aurea include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or oil to create a tincture or salve that can be used topically to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
